label: Superficial puncture wound without retained foreign body or infection
summary: Your symptoms fit a superficial puncture wound.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a superficial puncture wound. We did not find a retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, or another emergency problem today.
home: Keep the wound clean and protected.
Do not dig into the wound or try to remove anything at home.
meds: Take antibiotics only if your clinician prescribed them.
Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
return: Spreading redness, warmth, pus, red streaks, fever, or worsening swelling.
Numbness, weakness, severe pain, color change, or trouble moving the area.
New concern for a retained object, dirty wound, bite wound, or wound opening.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, wound care, or the ED if the wound is not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of superficial puncture wound.
No retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, bite wound, dirty/high-risk wound, diabetic-foot risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Retained foreign body, deep puncture, tendon/nerve/vessel/bone concern, joint violation, open fracture, bite wound, dirty wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, or delayed high-risk presentation.
Diabetic-foot risk, immunocompromise, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed wound/orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports cuts and puncture wound framing, deep-structure risk, infection warning signs, and higher-risk wound categories such as bites, dirty wounds, crush injuries, and foot wounds. Autonomous Phase 2101-2200 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed superficial puncture wound without retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, high-risk wound context, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of superficial puncture wound
no retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, high-risk wound context, or specialist-directed plan documented

label: Minor splinter removed without retained foreign body or infection
summary: Your clinician removed a small superficial splinter.
found: Your clinician removed the splinter and did not find a retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, or another emergency problem today.
home: Keep the area clean and covered if instructed.
Do not dig at the skin if it feels sore.
Protect the area from repeat irritation while it heals.
meds: Take antibiotics only if your clinician prescribed them.
Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
return: Spreading redness, warmth, pus, red streaks, fever, or worsening swelling.
Numbness, weakness, color change, severe pain, or trouble moving the area.
Feeling like something remains in the wound or symptoms not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, wound care, or the ED if pain, redness, or foreign-body concern is not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor superficial splinter removed.
No retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, bite/dirty wound, diabetic-foot risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Retained foreign body, glass/metal or deep object concern, tendon/nerve/vessel/bone concern, joint violation, open fracture, dirty wound, bite wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, or delayed high-risk presentation.
Diabetic-foot risk, immunocompromise, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed wound/hand plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports cuts and puncture wound framing, deep-structure risk, infection warning signs, and retained-object concerns. Autonomous Phase 2101-2200 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed minor superficial splinter removal without retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, high-risk wound context, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor superficial splinter removed
foreign body removed completely or no retained foreign body documented
no infection, deep structure injury, high-risk wound context, or specialist-directed plan documented

label: Healing wound without infection or dehiscence
summary: Your wound appears to be healing.
found: Your clinician checked the wound and documented that it is healing without infection, wound opening, or another emergency problem today.
home: Keep following the wound-care plan your clinician gave you.
Keep the wound clean and protected.
Do not soak, scrub, or pick at the healing tissue unless your clinician said it is safe.
meds: Take antibiotics only if your clinician prescribed them.
Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
return: Spreading redness, warmth, pus, red streaks, fever, or worsening swelling.
The wound opens, edges separate, bleeding will not stop, or pain becomes severe.
New numbness, weakness, color change, or concern for a deeper injury.
follow: Follow up as instructed for wound check, suture or staple care, or scar/healing concerns.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of healing wound or wound healing normally.
No infection, dehiscence/wound opening, bleeding problem, deep structure injury, high-risk wound context, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Wound infection, dehiscence, wound opening, uncontrolled bleeding, open fracture, tendon/nerve/vessel/bone concern, joint violation, bite wound, dirty wound, delayed high-risk presentation, severe pain, or neurovascular compromise.
Diabetic-foot risk, immunocompromise, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed wound plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports wound-healing education, keeping wounds clean/protected, following clinician dressing instructions, and escalation for increasing pain, redness, swelling, drainage, fever, or wound opening. Autonomous Phase 2101-2200 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-documented healing wound without infection, dehiscence, high-risk wound context, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of healing wound or wound healing normally
no infection, wound opening, deep structure injury, high-risk wound context, or specialist-directed plan documented

label: Uncomplicated ankle bruise without fracture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit an ankle bruise.
found: Your clinician diagnosed an uncomplicated ankle bruise. We did not find fracture concern, open wound, neurovascular compromise, infection, or another emergency problem today.
home: Rest from activity that worsens pain.
Use ice, elevation, compression, or support only as instructed.
Return to walking and activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: New numbness, color change, severe pain, or inability to walk.
Spreading redness, warmth, pus, fever, or worsening swelling.
New deformity, worsening pain, or symptoms not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, or orthopedics if pain, swelling, or walking is not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated ankle bruise/contusion.
No fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, inability to bear weight, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, inability to bear weight, high-energy trauma, crush injury, compartment syndrome concern, anticoagulation/bleeding risk, or unexplained bruising/abuse concern.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
must_not_miss: Bleeding disorder or unsafe bruising pattern.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports bruise/contusion framing, local care concepts, and escalation for severe pain, neurovascular symptoms, fracture concern, bleeding risk, or unusual bruising. Autonomous Phase 2101-2200 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated ankle bruise/contusion without fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, inability to bear weight, high-risk trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated ankle bruise/contusion
no fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-risk trauma, or specialist-directed plan documented

label: Uncomplicated shoulder bruise without fracture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit a shoulder bruise.
found: Your clinician diagnosed an uncomplicated shoulder bruise. We did not find fracture or dislocation concern, tendon rupture, open wound, neurovascular compromise, or another emergency problem today.
home: Rest from lifting or activity that worsens pain.
Use ice, heat, a sling, or gentle movement only as instructed.
Return to activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: New numbness, weakness, color change, severe pain, or inability to use the arm.
Spreading redness, warmth, pus, fever, or worsening swelling.
New deformity, chest pain, shortness of breath, or symptoms not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, or orthopedics if pain, motion, or strength is not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated shoulder bruise/contusion.
No fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, cardiopulmonary mimic, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, inability to use limb, high-energy trauma, crush injury, compartment syndrome concern, anticoagulation/bleeding risk, unexplained bruising/abuse concern, or cardiac/PE mimic.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
must_not_miss: Cardiopulmonary mimic when presentation is not clearly local trauma.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports bruise/contusion framing, local care concepts, and escalation for severe pain, neurovascular symptoms, fracture concern, bleeding risk, or unusual bruising. Autonomous Phase 2101-2200 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated shoulder bruise/contusion without fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-risk trauma, cardiopulmonary mimic, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated shoulder bruise/contusion
no fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-risk trauma, cardiopulmonary mimic, or specialist-directed plan documented
